title: aws-kms: ThrottlingException maps to Backend instead of Unavailable
description: In secretx-aws-kms, both sign() and public_key_der() map ALL service errors except NotFoundException to SecretError::Backend (permanent). This is wrong for KMS ThrottlingException and RequestThrottledException, which are transient and should map to Unavailable so callers know they can retry. The aws-sm backend correctly checks svc.meta().code() for ThrottlingException. Fix: add the same code check in the sign() and public_key_der() closures before falling through to Backend.
